style="text-align:left;margin-left:0;margin-right:0;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px;text-indent:0;vertical-align:middle;direction:ltr;"><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> a2:&quot;v ∈ set |q|&quot;<br/> </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>shows </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">&quot;the (priority q (min q)) ≤ the (priority q v)&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b><br/>## Informal proof </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><br/>Proof Step:<br/>	To prove this theorem, we proceed step by step:<br/>Step 1: Definitions Recap:<br/>	Let&prime;s recap the definitions of the relevant functions and properties:<br/>	- min Function: The min function returns the minimum value in the priority queue q. It is defined as `min q = fst (hd (alist_of q))`. ...<br/>	- priority Function: The priority function returns the priority of a value in the priority queue q. It is defined as `priority q v = map_of ...<br/>	- is_empty Function: The is_empty function checks whether the priority queue q is empty. It is defined as `is_empty q \&lt;equiv&gt;..<br/>Step 2: Proof Breakdown:To prove the theorem, we need to show that the priority of the minimum value in q is less than or equal to...<br/>Step 3: Case Analysis:<br/>	We will perform a case analysis based on the assumption a1: `\&lt;not&gt; is_empty q`. Since q is not empty, there are two possible cases:<br/>	Case 1: q is empty:<br/>	If q is empty according to the assumption, then it contradicts the premise that v is in the set of values of q (a2: `v \&lt;in&gt; set |q|`). ...<br/>	Case 2: q is not empty:<br/>	If q is not empty, then the minimum value in q can be obtained using the min function: `min q`. Let&prime;s denote this minimum value as m.<br/>Step 4: Priority of m and v:<br/>	We want to show that the priority of m is less than or equal to the priority of v. We can express this as: `the (priority q m) \&lt;le&gt; ...<br/>Step 5: Unfolding priority:<br/>	Using the definition of the priority function, we can rewrite the inequality as: `the (map_of (alist_of q) m) \&lt;le&gt; the (map_of (alist_of q) v)`.<br/>Step 6: Set Membership:<br/>	Since v is in the set of values of q according to the assumption a2, it implies that v is present in the list representation of q, which is ...<br/>Step 7: Map_of Behavior:<br/>	The map_of function returns None if the key v is not<br/><br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>Output:</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>## Formal proof</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">min_priority_leq_all:</font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New p.font:
Proof Step:
To prove this theorem, we proceed step by step:
Step 1: Definitions Recap:
Let′s recap the definitions of the relevant functions and properties:
- min Function: The min function returns the minimum value in the priority queue q. It is defined as `min q = fst (hd (alist_of q))`. ...
- priority Function: The priority function returns the priority of a value in the priority queue q. It is defined as `priority q v = map_of ...
- is_empty Function: The is_empty function checks whether the priority queue q is empty. It is defined as `is_empty q \<equiv>..
Step 2: Proof Breakdown:To prove the theorem, we need to show that the priority of the minimum value in q is less than or equal to...
Step 3: Case Analysis:
We will perform a case analysis based on the assumption a1: `\<not> is_empty q`. Since q is not empty, there are two possible cases:
Case 1: q is empty:
If q is empty according to the assumption, then it contradicts the premise that v is in the set of values of q (a2: `v \<in> set |q|`). ...
Case 2: q is not empty:
If q is not empty, then the minimum value in q can be obtained using the min function: `min q`. Let′s denote this minimum value as m.
Step 4: Priority of m and v:
We want to show that the priority of m is less than or equal to the priority of v. We can express this as: `the (priority q m) \<le> ...
Step 5: Unfolding priority:
Using the definition of the priority function, we can rewrite the inequality as: `the (map_of (alist_of q) m) \<le> the (map_of (alist_of q) v)`.
Step 6: Set Membership:
Since v is in the set of values of q according to the assumption a2, it implies that v is present in the list representation of q, which is ...
Step 7: Map_of Behavior:
The map_of function returns None if the key v is not
